behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local retry-policy math/state (attempt N -> delay), with no required module handoff. MOCK-MISS: not primarily an inter-module bug. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: real network/OS/subprocess is unnecessary; a pure in-process test can reproduce wrong doubling or cap logic exactly. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: Integration was considered, but behavior text is algorithmic ('doubles ... up to a configured maximum') and does not require transport/timer wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the backoff calculator/state object directly across consecutive failures (e.g., attempts 1..k), asserting exact exponential sequence and max-cap saturation.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module boundary: HTTP/error classification + retry policy handoff. MOCK-MISS: a unit test of either classifier or backoff alone can miss miswiring between them. REAL-INFRA: no real network required; synthetic HTTP responses in-process are sufficient. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no OS/network/subprocess requirement, (b) no AI output dependence, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM stage orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test retry orchestrator with a stub transport returning a 429-like response (and relevant headers/body), then assert it is recognized as rate-limit and scheduled delay equals expected policy/header-derived wait.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module behavior (timer/scheduler + request replay + response propagation). Unit tests on replay logic alone can miss incorrect original-request preservation or return-path wiring. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: fake clock + in-process stub transport can expose failures; real HTTP is not required. Rejection gate for System: behavior text does not require real network conditions, only correct resend and transparent return.
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test `call()` with first response as rate-limited, advance fake time to trigger retry, verify second transport invocation receives the same request payload, and assert caller gets successful result without extra wrapping.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is retry-loop counter + terminal error mapping handoff. A unit test of error class alone misses misclassification at orchestrator boundary. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed; repeated stubbed rate-limit responses reproduce it. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks pass (no System/Agentic/Workflow trigger).
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test `call()` with transport always returning rate-limit until retry budget is exhausted; assert final rejection is specifically `QuotaExhaustedError` (type/instance) and not a generic error.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly spans several local modules (retry state machine, backoff, quota accounting), so Unit is too low (MOCK-MISS). REAL-INFRA: can be validated in-process with fake timers and bounded attempts; no real OS/network required. Rejection gate for System: text requires chain termination logic, not real infrastructure faults.
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test end-to-end retry chain with persistent rate-limit responses, advance controlled time, assert attempt count and elapsed backoff progression stop at configured limit, and verify promise settles with terminal error (no infinite pending/loop).

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is shared-state coordination across callers/modules, not single-function logic. Unit tests on bucket math alone can miss orchestration races. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: in-process concurrent async calls with controlled stubs are enough to reveal incorrect per-caller isolation; real network is not inherently required by behavior text ('same process'). Rejection gate for System: no explicit OS/network/subprocess dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test by launching multiple concurrent `call()` invocations against one client/account, forcing quota pressure via stubbed responses, then assert a single shared quota counter/bucket governs all callers consistently.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: Ordering bugs arise at queue + dispatcher + async scheduling boundaries. Unit-testing queue container alone can miss real dispatch handoff errors. REAL-INFRA: deterministic in-process scheduler/fake timers can validate FIFO dispatch; no real HTTP/OS primitives required. Rejection gate for System: behavior specifies submission-order dispatch semantics, not real network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test by enqueueing several `call()` requests under quota-blocked conditions, then releasing capacity stepwise and asserting transport dispatch order matches original submission order exactly.
